title: Polybotrya marginata
taxonomy: Polybotrya marginata is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes many species of ferns. The order Polypodiales is further grouped into the class Polypodiopsida, which comprises various types of ferns and their allies. The class Polypodiopsida is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies. This classification places Polybotrya marginata within the plant kingdom, specifically within the division Pteridophyta.
uses: Polybotrya marginata is a species of fern with reported traditional uses in medicine, particularly in the Amazonian region. Its leaves and rhizomes are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. In traditional medicine, Polybotrya marginata is also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Additionally, its extracts have been studied for their potential antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Polybotrya marginata for medicinal purposes.
